skill.name: rarefriend-skills
skill.description: Cross-agent skills for Rarefriend personal CRM — search contacts, LinkedIn network, Google Calendar, Google Contacts, Microsoft Outlook email and calendar. Requires Rarefriend MCP. Use with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, or any Agent Skills-compatible client. Install per skill via npx skills add Whitefield-Labs/rarefriend-skills --skill <name>.
skill.summary: Cross-agent skills for Rarefriend personal CRM — search contacts, LinkedIn network, Google Calendar,...
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: After evaluation, the 781 static pattern hits are predominantly false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are triggered by the literal word 'secret' in OAuth client setup examples (e.g., RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_SECRET) — no actual cryptographic code is present. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' hits are markdown backtick characters used to format installation commands like `npx skills add` and `claude mcp add`. The 'hidden file in home directory' findings reference standard config paths such as `~/.cursor/mcp.json` and `~/.config/Claude/`. The 'hardcoded URL' findings are documentation links to rarefriend.com and the repository. No executable code, eval, dynamic imports, or credential exfiltration patterns were found. The skill is a documentation-only MCP skill package with no scripts. The files are all Markdown skill definitions and SETUP guides for the Rarefriend personal CRM integration.

content.user_title: Manage Your Network With AI-Powered CRM Skills
content.value_statement: Professional contacts are scattered across Google, LinkedIn, Microsoft, and your phone. Rarefriend skills unify them into one searchable CRM. Your AI assistant can search, tag, add notes, schedule meetings, and import contacts on your behalf.
actual_capabilities: Search and retrieve contacts from Google Contacts, Microsoft Outlook, LinkedIn, and manual entries through a unified interface
Create, update, and tag contacts with custom organization labels and relationship context
Log meeting notes, call summaries, and interaction history for each contact
Check Google Calendar and Microsoft Outlook availability and schedule events directly from conversations
Search and manage LinkedIn-synced connections using the Rarefriend Chrome extension
Import contacts in bulk from CSV files or connected integrations
limitations: Requires a Rarefriend account and OAuth credentials obtained from rarefriend.com
LinkedIn sync depends on the separate Rarefriend Chrome extension being installed and active
Calendar and email integration requires the Rarefriend MCP server to be running and connected
No offline mode — all operations require network access to the Rarefriend API

use_cases.title: Sales and business development
use_cases.description: Search your network for warm intros, find who you know at a target company, and log outreach activities.
use_cases.target_user: Founders, sales professionals, and business developers

use_cases.title: Personal relationship management
use_cases.description: Remember context about people you meet, track follow-ups, and never forget a conversation detail.
use_cases.target_user: Consultants, advisors, and networkers who value long-term relationships

use_cases.title: Executive and founder assistance
use_cases.description: Delegate contact lookups, meeting scheduling, and contact organization to your AI assistant.
use_cases.target_user: Executives, founders, and chief of staff roles

prompt_templates.title: Basic contact search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all contacts I have at Acme Corp

prompt_templates.title: Meeting note logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a meeting note for Jane Smith: discussed Q3 partnership opportunities, follow up next week about pilot program
prompt_templates.scenario: Capture conversation context after a meeting

prompt_templates.title: Calendar scheduling
prompt_templates.prompt: Schedule a 30-minute call with John Doe next Tuesday afternoon and send a calendar invite

prompt_templates.title: Bulk import and organize
prompt_templates.prompt: Import all contacts from my uploaded CSV file, tag anyone from the investor list as 'investor', and schedule a reminder to reach out to dormant contacts older than 6 months
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow combining import, tagging, and follow-up automation

output_examples.input: Find all contacts I have at Acme Corp
output: 1. Sarah Chen — VP Engineering (last interaction: 2 weeks ago, discussed API integration)
2. Mike Rodriguez — Product Manager (connected via LinkedIn, met at SaaStr 2024)
3. Lisa Park — Head of Partnerships (tagged: investor, warm intro from David)

output_examples.input: Who should I reach out to this week?
output: Based on your relationship history and tags, here are 4 suggested outreach targets:
1. Tom Wilson (investor) — last contact 45 days ago, overdue for update
2. Anna Liu (mentor) — quarterly check-in due this week
3. Chris Martinez (customer) — follow-up on Q2 renewal discussion
4. Priya Patel (recruiter) — intro requested by your network

best_practices: Always run the setup detection protocol first by calling list_connected_integrations before any CRM action to verify MCP is connected
Use specific search modes (name, company, role, email) when you know what you are looking for to get faster, more accurate results
Tag contacts consistently and add meeting notes promptly while context is fresh to build a useful relationship history
anti_patterns: Do not assume integrations are connected without calling list_connected_integrations first — always verify before performing actions
Do not use vague queries like 'find people' — use specific names, companies, or tags for reliable results
Do not skip meeting notes or interaction logging — undocumented contacts lose value over time

faq.question: What is Rarefriend?
faq.answer: Rarefriend is a personal CRM that unifies your contacts from Google, LinkedIn, Microsoft, and your phone. These skills let your AI assistant interact with that CRM through natural language.

faq.question: Do I need a Rarefriend account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a Rarefriend account and OAuth credentials (Client ID and Client Secret) from rarefriend.com under Settings, Integrations, MCP. The skills will not work without these credentials.

faq.question: Which AI clients are supported?
faq.answer: The skills work with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, and any client that supports the Agent Skills specification format. Each skill installs via npx skills add.

faq.question: How does LinkedIn sync work?
faq.answer: LinkedIn sync requires the separate Rarefriend Chrome extension to be installed. Once connected, your LinkedIn connections appear in the unified CRM alongside contacts from other sources.

faq.question: Can I install just one skill instead of all six?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --skill flag with npx skills add to install individual skills. The network-and-relationship-manager skill is recommended as a starting point since it provides access to all integrations.

faq.question: Is my data secure?
faq.answer: The skills themselves contain no executable code — they are Markdown documentation files. All data operations go through the official Rarefriend MCP server using your OAuth credentials. No data is sent to third parties.
